data.title: OpenAI's $500B Stargate venture mulls U.K. investment in future - report
data.description:
Stargate, a $500B U.S. datacenter project spearheaded by SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) and OpenAI, is mulling a future investment in the U.K. as it explores overseas locations to set up AI infrastructure, the Financial Times reported.

Britain has emerged as one of its top choices amid a recent push by the country's Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s to boost investment in the nation's growing AI industry, including a plan to bolster developers’ access to energy, the report added, citing people familiar with the matter.

Stargate's focus will remain on American infrastructure, but investment in the U.K. has been considered as part of a future international expansion if the project is successful, the report added.

Plans by the U.K. government to try to boost datacenter's access to electricity have attracted the project’s interest, the report noted.

Germany and France were also being seen as candidates for investment, according to the report. In February, France unveiled a €109B project in private investments in the AI ecosystem.

“As announced in January, Stargate remains fully committed to investing up to $500 billion over the next four years to build AI infrastructure in the United States," said OpenAI and SoftBank in a joint statement to Seeking Alpha.

Over the past few months, OpenAI has been lobbying governments in Europe to ease regulations and invest in data center infrastructure, the report noted.

Last week, the European Commission announced its plan to invest more in AI data centers and simplify rules around its AI Act.

In January, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son and Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) Co-Founder and Chairman Larry Ellison joined U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House to officially announce the $500B four-year initiative to build out AI infrastructure. OpenAI had said then that it would begin deploying $100B immediately for the Stargate Project.

Earlier this month, OpenAI announced a $40B new funding round, which included SoftBank, that valued the AI startup at $300B.

Oracle did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Blackstone Q1 earnings beat, as inflows rise
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) Q1 earnings beat the Wall Street consensus as the private equity firm pulled in its highest levels of inflows in almost three years amid a volatile market. Assets under management climbed to $1.17T.

"We also delivered positive investment performance across all of our major strategies," said Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman. "We are well positioned to navigate the current environment with $177B of dry powder to deploy and a resilient, capital-light business mode."

Its private equity infrastructure turned in the strongest performance of its strategies in the quarter.

Blackstone (NYSE:BX) stock rose 0.5% in Thursday premarket trading.

Net inflows of $61.6B compared with inflows of $57.5B in the prior quarter. 

Total assets under management of $1.17T at March, 31, 2025, topping the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.15T, rose from $1.13T at Dec. 31, 2024. Fee-earning AUM increased to $860.1B from $830.7B at the end of Q4 2024.

Q1 distributable EPS of $1.09, beating the consensus of $1.06, dropped from $1.69 in Q4 2024 and increased from $0.98 in last year’s Q1.

Q1 fee-related earnings of $1.26B, or $1.03 per share, beating the Visible Alpha consensus of $0.85, rose from $1.18B, or $0.96 per share, in Q4 and $1.16B, or $0.95 per share, in last year’s Q1.

Net management and advisory fees were $1.89B, up from $1.86B in the previous quarter and $1.71B a year ago.

At the end of the quarter, Blackstone (BX) had undrawn capital for investment, or dry powder, of $177.2B vs. $168.6B at the end of Q4. During the first quarter of 2025, the company deployed $36.4B of capital and recorded realizations of $25.5B.

Total expenses of $1.89B increased from $1.50B in Q4 and $1.79B in Q1 2024. Compensation and benefit expenses climbed to $1.43B from $1.04B in the prior quarter and $1.31B a year ago.

Q1 investment performance by segment (appreciation/gross returns):

  • Real Estate

    • Opportunistic: +0.2%

    • Core: +1.2% 

  • Private Equity
    • Corporate Private Equity: +1.1%

    • Tactical Opportunities: +2.9%

    • Secondaries: +0.6%

    • Infrastructure: +7.5%

  • Credit & Insurance

    • Private Credit: +2.7%

    • Liquid Credit: +0.5%

  • Multi-Asset Investing

    • Absolute Return Composite:+2.6%

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Blackstone Non-GAAP EPS of $1.09 beats by $0.03, revenue of $3.29B beats by $570M

data.title: Ally Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.16, revenue of $1.54B misses by $430M
data.description:
• Ally Financial press release (NYSE:ALLY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $1.54B (-22.6% Y/Y) misses by $430M.
  • Adjusted Total Net Revenue of $2.07B
  • $10.2 billion of consumer auto origination volume sourced from a record 3.8 million consumer auto applications.
  • Retail deposits of $146.1 billion were up $0.9 billion year over year, and up $2.6 billion quarter over quarter. Total deposits were $151.4 billion and Ally maintained an industry-leading customer retention rateG .
  • The average retail portfolio deposit rate was 3.75%, down 50 bps year over year and down 22 bps quarter over quarter.
  • Cash and cash equivalentsE totaled $9.5 billion. Highly liquid securities were $20.3 billion and unused pledged borrowing capacity at the FHLB and FRB was $11.3 billion and $26.9 billion, respectively.
  • Total current available liquidityF was $68.0 billion, equal to 5.7x uninsured deposit balances.

data.title: Trump administration orders halt to New York offshore wind project
data.description:
Interior Secretary Burgum late Wednesday ordered a halt to construction of Equinor's (NYSE:EQNR) Empire Wind project off the coast of New York, saying the Biden administration approved it without sufficient environmental analysis.

Burgum said he had consulted with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, whose department includes the National Marine Fisheries Service, which is involved in permitting offshore wind facilities.

The project, designed to power 500K homes, was scheduled to start commercial operation in 2027 and was fully permitted, New York Governor Kathy Hochul said.

The lease to Empire Wind, located 15-30 miles south of Long Island, initially was awarded to Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) in 2017 and is being developed in two separate projects; the project was approved by the Biden administration in November 2023, and construction started last year.

Equinor (EQNR) said in February it had already spent $2B on the project out of a planned $7B, with the rest expected to be covered by project financing and investment tax credits.

The company may have counted on ~$2B of financing to come from tax credits and those could be at risk even if the project was allowed to resume, according to Teodor Sveen-Nilsen, an analyst at Oslo-based Sparebank 1 Markets.

"Proceeding with the project without any tax credit, is probably a worst-case scenario for Equinor shareholders," Sveen-Nilsen said.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Blackstone beats Q1 estimates, inflows at $61.6 billion
FIS to sell Worldpay stake to Global Payments for $6.6B, to buy GPN's Issuer Solutions for $12B
data.description:
FIS (NYSE:FIS), or Fidelity National Information Services, agreed to sell its stake in Worldpay to Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) for $6.6B pretax value, and to acquire GPN's Issuer Solutions business for a net purchase price of $12B, the company said on Thursday.

"“We are pleased to fully monetize our Worldpay stake at an attractive valuation," said FIS President and CEO Stephanie Ferris. "The transaction enhances FIS’ financial profile and replaces our non-cash generating minority stake in Worldpay with a growing stream of recurring high-margin revenues and cash flows."

Issuer Solutions provides credit processing, with a client base in more than 75 countries. It processes more than 40B transactions annually and has partnerships with more than 170 financial institutions and corporations.

FIS (NYSE:FIS) expects long-term revenue synergies of more than $125M from the acquisition. It also expects the deal to add to adjusted EBITDA margins, adjusted EPS, and adjusted cash flow in the first 12 months, and generate EBITDA synergies of more than $150M by the third year.

The company will fund the Issuer Solutions acquisition through a combination of $8B of new debt and the value from the sale of the minority stake in Worldpay. After the closing of the deals, FIS (FIS) expects pro forma gross leverage to be ~3.4x, deleveraging to its target gross leverage of 2.8x within 18 months.

The deals are expected to close in H1 2026.

FIS (FIS) expects to report Q1 adjusted EBITDA of ~$1.21, at the high end of its outlook and topping the $1.20 consensus estimate. Q1 revenue is expected to be $2.5B, in line with the consensus.

The company also reaffirmed its 2025 outlook for adjusted revenue growth, adjusted EBITDA, and adjusted EPS.

FIS (FIS) stock slipped 0.9% and Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) stock dropped 4.2% in premarket trading.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.
